title: Salsa Verde Black Bean Enchiladas
description: HelloFresh Hall of Fame — flour tortillas stuffed with a Southwestern-spiced filling of black beans, tomatoes, and roasted poblano, coated in tangy green salsa and topped with melted Monterey Jack cheese. A hearty, crowd-pleasing bake.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Dice onion and poblano. Dice tomatoes. Mince garlic. Drain and rinse black beans.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and poblano; cook 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic, cumin, chili powder, and smoked paprika; cook 1 minute.
Add black beans and tomatoes. Season with salt. Cook 3–4 minutes, stirring, until heated through and slightly thickened. Remove from heat.
Spread ½ cup salsa verde on the bottom of a 9x13 baking dish.
Spoon about ½ cup of the bean filling down the center of each tortilla. Sprinkle with a little cheese. Roll up tightly and place seam-side down in the baking dish.
Pour remaining salsa verde evenly over the enchiladas. Sprinkle with remaining Monterey Jack cheese.
Bake uncovered for 20–25 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly and the edges are lightly golden.
Serve topped with sour cream, fresh cilantro, and a squeeze of lime.
tips: Warm tortillas briefly in the microwave (30 seconds wrapped in a damp paper towel) before rolling — they'll be more pliable and won't crack.
Add shredded rotisserie chicken to the filling if you want a non-vegetarian version.
Corn tortillas can be used for a more traditional enchilada — dip them in warm salsa verde before rolling.
These reheat beautifully the next day — cover with foil and warm at 350°F for 15 minutes.
